In this method, the user is presented wi: In this method, the user is presented with an image containing distorted text they must identify. The User is then required to enter the characters visible in the image to submit the form.
In this method, website visitors are req: In this method, website visitors are required to solve a simple arithmetic problem before they can submit the form.

Rule-based protection (CAPTCHA, Timer, I: Rule-based protection (CAPTCHA, Timer, IP) catches simple bots but misses advanced ones like headless Chrome or Puppeteer. SilentShield API uses AI behavior analysis, browser fingerprinting, and adaptive challenges to detect 99% of bots — invisibly, without CAPTCHAs.
See the difference since activating API: See the difference since activating API protection.
Select the plugins that should be protec: Select the plugins that should be protected. You can enable multiple or only single elements. It is also possible to disable the protection for single formulars using hooks. Have a look at the documentation for further information
Settings are inherited from the parent l: Settings are inherited from the parent level. The hierarchy is: Global > Integration > Form. You only need to set values where you want to deviate from the parent.
Share SilentShield with other WordPress: Share SilentShield with other WordPress users. When they sign up using your referral link, you both get 1 month free on any paid plan.

SilentShield has blocked %d spam submiss: SilentShield has blocked %d spam submissions so far. Want to catch even more? The SilentShield API detects advanced bots that rule-based protection misses.
SilentShield updated to v%s! New feature: SilentShield updated to v%s! New features include enhanced analytics, detailed block tracking, and reason codes. For even stronger protection, try the SilentShield API with AI behavior analysis.
Something went wrong, please try again l: Something went wrong, please try again later or contact the plugin author.
Storing personal data (email addresses,: Storing personal data (email addresses, IP addresses) in plain text may require a legal basis under GDPR Art. 6. Use this setting only for temporary debugging purposes to identify false-positive blocks. Disable it again after troubleshooting. Ensure your privacy policy covers the storage of submission data. We recommend keeping the retention period as short as possible while this setting is active.
Supported ids: avada, fluentform, elemen: Supported ids: avada, fluentform, elementor, cf7, wpforms, ultimatemember, gravityforms, wordpress_comments, wordpress, woocommerce.

The <strong>placeholder</strong> will be: The placeholder will be displayed within the captcha input field.
The BBCode Limiter allows you to disable: The BBCode Limiter allows you to disable BBCode in your forms. BBCode, which stands for Bulletin Board Code, is a lightweight markup language used to format posts in many message boards, online forums, and comment sections. BBCode tags are similar to HTML but are simpler and safer.
The Browser has been identified as crawl: The Browser has been identified as crawler/bot. Submission has been blocked.
The IP of the submitter has been blocked: The IP of the submitter has been blocked. This happens if the user has to often submitted forms or has been identified multiple times as spammer.
The JavaScript validation failed. This i: The JavaScript validation failed. This indicates that the form was submitted by a bot or a skript that could not run our validation.
The Limit %d for URLs has been reached. Remove the %s to continue.
The Limit %d has been reached. Remove th: The Limit %d has been reached. Remove the %s to continue.
The SilentShield API adds AI-powered beh: The SilentShield API adds AI-powered behavior analysis that catches bots your current protection misses — completely invisible to your users.
The URL Limiter is limiting the number o: The URL Limiter is limiting the number of hyperlinks that can be included in the content of a form submission. Keep in mind, that the limit is by field not by form.
The blacklist is a list of prohibited or: The blacklist is a list of prohibited or undesirable input values. When a user submits a form, the data provided is checked against the blacklist. If any part of the users input matches an entry on the blacklist, the form submission will be rejected and the user will be asked to provide different information.
The custom error message will be display: The custom error message will be displayed for website visitors if the error appears, therefor it would be helpful to explain them how to solve this issue
The defined timer failed. The form was s: The defined timer failed. The form was submitted to often / to fast. This can be caused by bots trying to send the form with multiple data in a short period of time.
The field for validation is missing. Tha: The field for validation is missing. That can be caused by bots not allowing javascript to work.
The form has been blocked by given rules: The form has been blocked by given rules (BBCode, Blacklist or URL).
The protection for avada was verified. T: The protection for avada was verified. The form has been submitted.
The protection for cf7 was verified. The: The protection for cf7 was verified. The form has been submitted.
The protection for the comment was verif: The protection for the comment was verified. The form has been submitted.
The time verification failed. This is ca: The time verification failed. This is caused if the form is submitted to fast after page load.
The user could not complete the captcha. Validation failed.
The user will not be able to submit any: The user will not be able to submit any forms until he gets unblocked after the given amount of seconds.
The word %s is blacklisted. Please remov: The word %s is blacklisted. Please remove it to continue.
There are multiple protection mechanism: There are multiple protection mechanism available that you can use to stop incoming spam. Feel free to enable / disable them as required.
These counters show the overall protecti: These counters show the overall protection activity since the plugin has been activated.

When enabled, email addresses and IP add: When enabled, email addresses and IP addresses are stored in plain text in the submission logs instead of being masked. This allows you to identify which user was blocked and contact them if needed.
When enabled, every blocked submission i: When enabled, every blocked submission is logged with a machine-readable reason code and a human-readable explanation of why it was blocked. This allows you to analyze exactly which protection methods are working and why.
When enabled, the plugin tracks all loca: When enabled, the plugin tracks all local protection checks and estimates how many additional bots the SilentShield API would have caught. No data is sent externally — all analysis is local. View results on the Analytics page.

Automatically checks whether the browser: Automatically checks whether the browser can execute JavaScript. Real visitors do not notice anything. Bots that submit forms without a browser are blocked.
Before using the API for decisions, you: Before using the API for decisions, you can enable Shadow Mode. API results are only logged but not used for blocking. This lets you compare how the API performs versus local modules.
Blocks requests without a valid browser: Blocks requests without a valid browser (User-Agent). Normal visitors are never affected.
Blocks spam words, links and BBCode: Blocks spam words, links and BBCode
Blocks submissions containing specific w: Blocks submissions containing specific words. Greedy mode also catches partial matches.
Blocks submissions containing specific words or phrases. Must be enabled first under Settings > Content Rules.

If the API is unreachable, all local pro: If the API is unreachable, all local protection modules continue to work normally. There is no loss of protection. A notice appears in the admin area.

Logs all administrative changes: setting: Logs all administrative changes: settings modified, plugin activated/deactivated, API errors, rate limit hits.
Measures the time between page load and: Measures the time between page load and form submission. If a form is submitted in under 0.5 seconds (impossible for humans), it is blocked.

Prerequisite: Detailed tracking must be: Prerequisite: Detailed tracking must be enabled under Settings.
Prevents the same form from being submit: Prevents the same form from being submitted multiple times in a row (e.g. by double-clicking).
Shows at a glance: number of checked, al: Shows at a glance: number of checked, allowed and blocked submissions, active protection modules, and recent blocks.
SilentShield automatically protects your: SilentShield automatically protects your WordPress forms from spam and bots. The plugin works invisibly in the background and combines multiple protection mechanisms:
SilentShield is GDPR compliant: no cooki: SilentShield is GDPR compliant: no cookies, no tracking, no external services (except the optional SilentShield API).
Stores IP addresses only as hashes (not: Stores IP addresses only as hashes (not in plain text) in logs
Switch the captcha method from Honeypot: Switch the captcha method from Honeypot to Math or Image
The API runs alongside all local protect: The API runs alongside all local protection features. Captcha, timer, JS protection etc. remain active. The API adds an additional layer of protection.
The Mail Log stores personal data. Make: The Mail Log stores personal data. Make sure this is compatible with your privacy policy. All logs are automatically deleted after the configured retention period (default: 30 days).
The SilentShield API is an optional clou: The SilentShield API is an optional cloud service for advanced bot detection. It analyzes visitor behavior (mouse movements, typing patterns etc.) and detects bots more reliably.
The SilentShield API is optional and mus: The SilentShield API is optional and must be explicitly enabled
The whitelist always takes priority over: The whitelist always takes priority over the blacklist. If an IP is on both lists, it will be allowed through.
Tip: Honeypot mode is completely invisib: Tip: Honeypot mode is completely invisible to visitors - ideal if you do not want a visible captcha.
Use the SilentShield API for behavior-ba: Use the SilentShield API for behavior-based detection
What can be customized per form?: What can be customized per form?
What happens during an API outage?: What happens during an API outage?
When enabled, limits how often an IP add: When enabled, limits how often an IP address can submit forms.
When enabled, logs all form submissions: When enabled, logs all form submissions (sent and blocked) with sender, recipient and content.
Whitelisted users, IP addresses or email: Whitelisted users, IP addresses or email addresses skip all protection checks. They will never see a captcha and will never be blocked.
You can customize protection settings fo: You can customize protection settings for individual integrations or even individual forms, without changing the global settings.
